summary: Regression test after trimming backend/requirements.txt to 12 packages. Re-ran existing pytest suites (backend_test.py + test_new_features.py = 30 tests) and additionally verified the SFTP /test endpoint. Result: 29/30 pass. All runtime imports work (fastapi, motor, pymongo, bs4+lxml, paramiko, python-multipart, bcrypt, pyjwt). No ImportError anywhere. The single failing test is a pre-existing incorrect assertion, NOT a regression from the dependency trim.

minor.issue: Test 'test_set_and_get_sftp_persists' expects has_password=false after PUT with blank password, but server intentionally preserves existing password when blank is sent (server.py L374-376). Test assertion is outdated — server behavior is correct/desired UX. Fix the test, not the server.

action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) Update test_new_features.py::test_set_and_get_sftp_persists — the server correctly preserves existing password on blank submit; the assertion `has_password is False` after blank PUT is incorrect. Either remove that block or assert `has_password is True` (documents intended UX).
critical_code_review_comments: requirements.txt trim is clean and minimal — matches server.py imports exactly (fastapi, uvicorn, dotenv, pymongo/motor, pydantic, jwt, bcrypt, multipart, bs4, lxml, paramiko). No dead imports.

SFTP /test endpoint correctly uses socket.create_connection with timeout before paramiko handshake — verified: unreachable host returns cleanly in ~15s (not hanging). Blank config short-circuits in <100ms.
server.py handles blank-password PUT gracefully (preserves existing). Good UX. Just needs the test updated to match.
test_credentials: admin@wifetobe.org / ChangeMe!2026 — verified working, login 200 with httpOnly cookie.
seed_data_creation: None — used seeded wifetobe site and admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dependency trim regression is CLEAN. All 12 packages sufficient. SFTP /test verified fast + safe (15s socket timeout, no hang). Only outstanding item is a stale test assertion in test_new_features.py L110 — cosmetic, unrelated to the fix under review. Publish returns published=false with 'SFTP not configured' — expected in sandbox (no Hostinger creds).
rca of the issue: Test expects blank password PUT to clear the stored password. Server (server.py:374-376) intentionally treats blank password as 'keep existing' — a deliberate UX choice so admins don't have to re-enter the password every edit. The test contradicts server intent. Recommend updating the test to assert the preservation behavior.
